mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a high threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that tightens financial conditions, increases the equity risk premium, and negatively impacts corporate profitability, leading to lower forward equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 10-year lookback.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust labor market and strong underlying economic activity. This signals higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity market returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 1-month changes in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales growth and an increase in the unemployment rate indicates a contraction in consumer spending power and confidence. This joint signal points to an imminent economic slowdown, negatively impacting corporate revenues and equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2 and 3-month change in RSAFS < 0
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in over 50% of periods where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in industrial production is a direct measure of output in the manufacturing, mining, and utility sectors. Positive momentum in this series signals expanding economic output and corporate profitability, supporting higher equity prices.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated, any increase in the federal funds rate has a magnified negative impact on equities. This is because the central bank's commitment to fighting inflation implies a higher probability of inducing an economic slowdown, compressing valuation multiples.
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPI is above 3%, the correlation between changes in FEDFUNDS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below a key threshold indicates a very tight labor market, which fuels wage inflation concerns. This increases the likelihood of hawkish central bank policy, raising discount rates and creating a negative outlook for equities.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower when UNRATE is below 4.0% compared to when it is above 4.0% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key leading indicator of economic activity and consumer confidence. A positive surprise or strong momentum in HOUST signals robust demand and investment, which positively impacts market sentiment and short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of 1-month changes in HOUST for 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monetary policy is already restrictive, a deceleration in real economic growth is particularly bearish for equities. The high cost of capital limits firms' ability to weather a slowdown, leading to a sharp downward revision of earnings expectations.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, the correlation between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.
